name: Vaswani et al., 'Attention Is All You Need', NeurIPS 2017

task.description: Create a single self-contained 1920x1080 HTML slide (inline CSS + inline SVG, no scrolling) titled 'Attention Is All You Need (Vaswani et al., 2017)'. Lay out FOUR coexisting regions that share a common top edge and a common bottom edge: (A) LEFT panel (33% width): an inline-SVG encoder-decoder stack diagram with two columns of stacked sublayers - encoder column: 'Input Embedding' -> 'Positional Encoding' -> 'Multi-Head Attention' -> 'Add & Norm' -> 'Feed Forward' -> 'Add & Norm' (label the stack 'Nx = 6'); decoder column: 'Output Embedding' -> 'Positional Encoding' -> 'Masked Multi-Head Attention' -> 'Add & Norm' -> 'Multi-Head Attention' -> 'Add & Norm' -> 'Feed Forward' -> 'Add & Norm' -> 'Linear' -> 'Softmax'. (B) CENTER panel (33%): the two equations 'Attention(Q,K,V) = softmax(QK^T / sqrt(d_k)) V' and 'MultiHead(Q,K,V) = Concat(head_1,...,head_h) W^O' rendered with real superscripts/subscripts, plus a 4-item bullet list of contributions (no recurrence/convolution; parallelizable; O(1) path length; state-of-the-art BLEU). (C) RIGHT panel (34%): a results table with header row and rows: 'EN-DE BLEU 28.4', 'EN-FR BLEU 41.8', 'Training cost 3.5 days x 8 P100', 'Params (base) 65M', 'Params (big) 213M'. (D) a one-line footnote pinned to the bottom edge citing the paper. All text is provided; reproduce it faithfully. ALSO add to panel C the positional-encoding formula 'PE(pos,2i)=sin(pos/10000^(2i/d_model))' with real sub/superscripts, and expand the results table to 6 rows by adding 'Heads h=8'.
requirements: No element overlaps any other element; no SVG arrow passes through any box's label text
The three panels A, B, C have the same top edge and the same bottom edge (equal panel heights, aligned)
The three panels are equal-width within a few percent and separated by a visible uniform gutter (>=24px)
Every encoder and decoder sublayer box listed is present and its label is fully inside the box (no clipping)
Both equations render QK^T and W^O as superscripts and d_k as a subscript (not plain inline text)
All five result-table rows are present with both label and value, no cell text truncated
The four contribution bullets are each at most two lines and fully inside panel B
Nothing overflows the 1920x1080 canvas; there is no horizontal or vertical scrollbar
The footnote is fully visible above the bottom edge and does not overlap any panel
Every text element is at least 12px
The positional-encoding formula renders its superscript (2i/d_model) and is fully inside panel C, not clipped
task.paper: Vaswani et al., 'Attention Is All You Need', NeurIPS 2017

ss_html:
Attention Is All You Need (Vaswani et al., 2017)

Transformer Architecture

Key Equations

Attention(Q,K,V) = softmax(QKT / √dk) V
MultiHead(Q,K,V) = Concat(head1,...,headh) WO

Key Contributions

  • No recurrence or convolution operations
  • Fully parallelizable training
  • O(1) sequential path length
  • State-of-the-art BLEU scores

Results & Details

PE(pos,2i) = sin(pos/10000(2i/dmodel))
Metric Value
EN-DE BLEU 28.4
EN-FR BLEU 41.8
Training cost 3.5 days × 8 P100
Params (base) 65M
Params (big) 213M
Heads h 8
Vaswani, A., Shazeer, N., Parmar, N., Uszkoreit, J., Jones, L., Gomez, A. N., ... & Polosukhin, I. (2017). Attention is all you need. NIPS.
rv_html:
Attention Is All You Need (Vaswani et al., 2017)

Transformer Architecture

Key Formulations

Attention(Q,K,V) = softmax(QKT / √dk) V
MultiHead(Q,K,V) = Concat(head1,...,headh) WO

Key Contributions

  • No recurrence or convolution operations
  • Fully parallelizable training
  • O(1) sequential path length
  • State-of-the-art BLEU scores

Results & Details

PE(pos,2i) = sin(pos/10000(2i/dmodel))
Metric Value
EN-DE BLEU 28.4
EN-FR BLEU 41.8
Training cost 3.5 days × 8 P100
Params (base) 65M
Params (big) 213M
Heads h 8
Vaswani, A., Shazeer, N., Parmar, N., Uszkoreit, J., Jones, L., Gomez, A. N., ... & Polosukhin, I. (2017). Attention is all you need. Advances in neural information processing systems, 30.

For the transformer diagram, I'm planning to stack the encoder layers (input embedding, positional encoding, attention, normalization, and feed forward) alongside the decoder layers (which include masked attention, cross-attention, and additional normalization steps) within the left panel's constraints. Now I'm setting up the HTML structure with a full-screen canvas, establishing the title area at 80px, the footnote at 40px, and calculating the remaining space for the three main panels to fit within the 910px height. I'll start writing the HTML with careful dimension handling. For the results table, I need six rows covering the BLEU scores, training metrics, and parameter counts.
